Title: Overexpression of petunia chalcone isomerase in tomato results in fruit containing increased levels of flavonols

Genes.Function: Catalyzes the isomerization of naringenin chalcone to naringenin, a key step in flavonol biosynthesis
Genes.ExperimentalEvidence: Transgenic overexpression in tomato led to 78-fold increase in peel flavonols (mainly rutin), depletion of naringenin chalcone substrate, and accumulation of quercetin and kaempferol glycosides
Genes.Phenotype: Increased flavonol content in fruit peel (up to 78-fold), no gross phenotypical differences, stable inheritance over 4 generations
Genes.TissueSpecificity: Fruit peel-specific accumulation despite constitutive expression
Genes.MetabolicEffect: Increased antioxidant capacity, potential health benefits
